extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE attacked on final Mur de Huy ascent, overtook VAN ENDERT and held off BETTIOL to end VALVERDE's four-year winning streak
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE waited until final Mur ascent to attack, departing from typical early-move strategy that characterized his 2015-2016 podiums.
Quick-Step sent LANDA in early breakaway at first Mur crossing, forcing rivals to chase while ALAPHILIPPE preserved energy.
BETTIOL showed unexpected climbing punch to finish third, marking him as dangerous late-race aggressor on steep finale walls.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: third podium transformed to victory by delaying final move to last 200m versus premature 2015-2016 attacks
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: four-peat winner beaten despite favored status, suggesting vulnerability when opponents delay attacks until steepest pitch
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: positive: Milan-Sanremo winner's third place confirms he can sustain efforts on repeated steep walls, not just one-day flat classics
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: early breakaway role at first Mur crossing reveals Quick-Step willing to sacrifice GC riders as tactical pawns
